zh.lifeSummary: 當Emma du Bois-l'Évêque於1022年7月11日出生在法國豪-諾門迪瑟-馬利波-利艾e可時，她的父親Ibert Marshall de Bec47歲，母親Avoline de Menuz32歲。她和Hugh de Lacy Lord of Lassy至少有1個女兒。她去世於1074年1月28日在法國巴-諾門迪卡爾瓦多斯隆維爾享年51歲，埋葬在法國哈帕格-亞登芒內艾白內艾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Emma du Bois-l'Évêque于1022年7月11日出生在法国豪-諾門迪瑟-馬利波-利艾e可时，她的父亲Ibert Marshall de Bec47岁，母亲Avoline de Menuz32岁。她和Hugh de Lacy Lord of Lassy至少有1个女儿。她去世于1074年1月28日在法国巴-諾門迪卡爾瓦多斯隆維爾享年51岁，埋葬在法国哈帕格-亞登芒內艾白內艾。

nameMeanings.name: du
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Hong, Jian, Jing, Li, Ping, Yan, Yang, Yong, Bin, Chan, Cheng.  Vietnamese Thanh, Quang, Cuong, Dung, Hung, Binh, Minh, Duong, Hao, Hien, Liem, Lien.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 杜, meaning ‘birchleaf pear’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by descendants of Du Kang (杜康), an official noted as a winemaker during the reign of the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (ii) from the placename Du (杜), the name of a city (located in Shaanxi province) granted to Du Bo (杜伯, the Earl of Du), head of the state of Du (杜), who was eventually killed by King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782 BC ). After that, the state of Du (杜) was annexed and the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (iii) adopted as a surname by the Du Gu Hun (獨孤渾) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 都, meaning ‘capital’ in Chinese: (i) from the personal name Zi Du (子都), style name of Gong-Sun Yu, an official in the state of Zheng during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) shortened form of the compound surname Gong Du (公都), which is sometimes traced back to Tian, a prince in the state of Chu who was granted the fief Du (都), or alternatively to Gong Du Zi (公都子), an official in the state of Qi.

variants.name: Doo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Jin Ho, Young, Jong, Ling, Mi Sook, Quong, Yi.
English (Surrey): probably a variant of   Dew  .

variants.name: Duh
meanings: Slovenian and Croatian: nickname from duh ‘spirit, ghost’.

variants.name: Do
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Minh, Thanh, Hien, Long, Dung, Anh, Tuan, Hoang, Hai, Quang, Son.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Chi, Sang, Dong, Chan, Han, Ho, Bai, Chien, Lai, Man.
Vietnamese (Đỗ): from the Chinese surname 杜, see Du 1.
Vietnamese (Đồ): from the Chinese surname 涂 (or 塗), see Tu 1.

variants.name: Dou
meanings: Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 竇, meaning ‘hole’ in ancient Chinese: (i) adopted as a surname by Yue Zhu and Yue Long, two sons of Shao Kang, the sixth king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ). Yue Zhu and Yue Long are said to have adopted Dou (竇) (meaning ‘hole’) as a surname because their pregnant grandmother had escaped from a rebellion through a hole in the wall before giving birth to their father, Shao Kang, who later recaptured the throne. (ii) traced back to the Dou (竇) family from the Di ethnic group and some other families from the Xianbei ethnic group in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 豆, denoting an ancient stemmed cup of bowl: (i) traced back to some inhabitants in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) adopted as the surname by the Chi Xiao Dou (赤小豆) and Dou Lu (豆盧) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 鈄, referring to a type of ancient vessel for drinking wine: said to be borne by the descendants of the Duke Kang of Qi (died 379  BC ), who was ousted from his throne in the state of Qi and sent into exile. It was said that he was forced to use the vessel Dou (鈄) to cook food during his exile, and some of his descendants inherited Dou as the surname to memorize this period.

variants.name: Yu
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Ming, Yong, Young, Ping, Chi, Chun, Jae, Li, Sung, Jin, Yang.  Korean Chung, Chang, Byung, Chong, Min, Pyong, Myong, Hyong, Moon, Yiu, Inyoung, Shiu.

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 余 (or rarely written as 餘), meaning ‘I’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the second element of the personal name You Yu (由余), the name of a prime minister in the state of Qin during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from Yu (余), the name of a small state during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) said to be borne by descendants of Xia Yu, the first king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 于 (meaning ‘go’ in ancient Chinese) or 於 (often simplified as 于 in Mainland China, meaning ‘depend on’ in ancient Chinese): (i) simplified form of the Chinese character 邘, the name of an ancient state (located in Qinyang in Henan province), which was granted to Yu Shu (邘叔), the third son of King Wen of Zhou (1152–1056 BC ). After it was annexed by the state of Zheng during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ), the Chinese character 邘 (pronounced Yu) was simplified to (于) and adopted as a surname. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Wan Niu Yu (萬忸于) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ). (iii) adopted in place of the Chinese compound surname Chun-Yu (淳于), because the first element of the surname, Chun, happened to share the same pronunciation with the personal name of the emperor Li Chun, also known as Emperor Xianzong of Tang (778–820 AD ), which was taboo in ancient China. (iv) said to be traced back to Yu Ze (於則), who said to be the inventor of shoes, an official the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (v) from the second element of the placename Shang Yu (商於) in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ).

nameMeanings.name: Emma
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Old French name, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, originally a short form of compound names such as Ermintrude containing the word erm(en), irm(en) ‘entire’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, but its popularity in medieval England was greatly enhanced by the fact that it had been borne by the mother of Edward the Confessor, herself a Norman. In modern times, it was only in moderate use early in the 20th century but rose sharply in favour in the 1970s and has since remained perennially popular.

en.lifeSummary: When Emma du Bois-l'Évêque was born on 11 July 1022, in Bois-l'Évêque, Seine-Maritime, Upper Normandy, France, her father, Ibert Marshall de Bec, was 47 and her mother, Avoline de Menuz, was 32. She had at least 1 daughter with Hugh de Lacy Lord of Lassy. She died on 28 January 1074, in Longueville, Calvados, Lower Normandy, France, at the age of 51, and was buried in Épernay, Marne, Champagne-Ardenne, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Emma du Bois-l'Évêque est née le 11 juillet 1022, à Bois-l'Évêque, Seine-Maritime, Haute-Normandie, France, son père, Ibert Marshall de Bec, avait 47 ans et sa mère, Avoline de Menuz, avait 32 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fille avec Hugh de Lacy Lord of Lassy. Elle est décédée le 28 janvier 1074, à Longueville, Calvados, Basse-Normandie, France, à l’âge de 51 ans, et a été enterrée à Épernay, Marne, Champagne-Ardenne, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Emma du Bois-l'Évêque am 11. Juli 1022 in Bois-l'Évêque, Seine-Maritime, Haute-Normandie, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Ibert Marshall de Bec 47 und ihre Mutter Avoline de Menuz 32. Sie hatte mit Hugh de Lacy Lord of Lassy mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 28. Januar 1074 im Alter von 51 Jahren in Longueville, Calvados, Basse-Normandie, Frankreich und wurde in Épernay, Marne, Champagne-Ardenne, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Emma du Bois-l'Évêque è nata il il 11 luglio 1022 nel Bois-l'Évêque, Seine-Maritime, Haute-Normandie, Francia, sua padre, Ibert Marshall de Bec, aveva 47 anni e sua madre, Avoline de Menuz, aveva 32 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlia con Hugh de Lacy Lord of Lassy. Morì il 28 gennaio 1074, nel Longueville, Calvados, Basse-Normandie, Francia, all'età di 51 anni, e fu sepolta a Épernay, Marne, Champagne-Ardenne, Francia.

ja.Bois-l'Évêque, Seine-Maritime, Haute-Nor: フランスハウテ-ノーマンディセアイネ-マリティメボイス-ル'eveク
ja.Longueville, Calvados, Basse-Normandie,: フランスバセ-ノーマンディカルヴァドスロングエヴィル
ja.Falaise, Calvados, Normandie, France: フランスノーマンディカルヴァドスファレイゼ
ja.Épernay, Marne, Champagne-Ardenne, Franc: フランスチャンプアグネ-アルデンマルンeペルナイ
ja.lifeSummary: Emma du Bois-l'Évêqueが1022年7月11日にフランスハウテ-ノーマンディセアイネ-マリティメボイス-ル'eveクで生まれた時，父Ibert Marshall de Becは47歳，母Avoline de Menuzは32歳であった。彼女はHugh de Lacy Lord of Lassyとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1074年1月28日にフランスバセ-ノーマンディカルヴァドスロングエヴィルで51歳で死去し，フランスチャンプアグネ-アルデンマルンeペルナイにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Emma du Bois-l'Évêque(이)가 1022년 7월 11일 프랑스 하테-노먼디 세이인-마이팀 보이스-'e브e크에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Ibert Marshall de Bec(은)는 47세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Avoline de Menuz(은)는 32세였다. 그녀는 Hugh de Lacy Lord of Lassy(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1074년 1월 28일 프랑스 배세-노먼디 캘바도스 론구에빌에서 51세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 챔파그니-아르데니 마니 e퍼나이에 묻혔다.

mn.11 July 1022–28 January 1074: 1022 оны Долоодугаар сарын 11–1074 оны Нэгдүгээр сарын 28

pt.11 July 1022–28 January 1074: 11 de julho de 1022–28 de janeiro de 1074
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Emma du Bois-l'Évêque nasceu em 11 de julho de 1022, em Bois-l'Évêque, Seine-Maritime, Haute-Normandie, França, seu pai, Ibert Marshall de Bec, tinha 47 anos e sua mãe, Avoline de Menuz, tinha 32 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filha com Hugh de Lacy Lord of Lassy. Ela faleceu em 28 de janeiro de 1074, em Longueville, Calvados, Basse-Normandie, França, com 51 anos, e foi sepultada em Épernay, Marne, Champagne-Ardenne, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Emma du Bois-l'Évêque родилась 11 июля 1022 г. в местности боис-л'eвeку, сине-маритим, хауте-норманди, Франция, её отцу по имени Ibert Marshall de Bec было 47 лет, а матери, которую звали Avoline de Menuz, 32. У неё была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Hugh de Lacy Lord of Lassy. Она умерла 28 января 1074 г. в местности лонгуевилл, калвадос, бассе-норманди, Франция в возрасте 51 год и была похоронена в местности eпирнеи, марн, чампагне-арденн, Франция.

es.11 July 1022–28 January 1074: 11 de julio de 1022–28 de enero de 1074
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Emma du Bois-l'Évêque nació el 11 de julio de 1022, en Bois-l'Évêque, Seine-Maritime, Haute-Normandie, Francia, su papá, Ibert Marshall de Bec, tenía 47 años y su mamá, Avoline de Menuz, tenía 32 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Hugh de Lacy Lord of Lassy. Murió el 28 de enero de 1074, en Longueville, Calvados, Basse-Normandie, Francia, a la edad de 51 años, y fue sepultada en Épernay, Marne, Champagne-Ardenne, Francia.
